prompt: Our website uses the following colour combinations. Which ones fail WCAG 2.1 AA contrast requirements and what should we replace them with? Body text #767676/#FFFFFF, Nav links #0066CC/#FFFFFF, Button text #FFFFFF/#3D9970, Placeholder #AAAAAA/#FFFFFF, Error messages #CC0000/#FFFFFF, Disabled button #999999/#CCCCCC.

expectations.text: Correctly identifies SC 1.4.3 as the applicable criterion with level AA
expectations.evidence: Does not cite SC 1.4.3 by number; only references WCAG 2.1 AA generically

expectations.text: Correctly flags button text (#FFFFFF on #3D9970) as failing
expectations.evidence: Correctly identified as failing with ~3.51:1 ratio

expectations.text: Correctly flags placeholder text (#AAAAAA on #FFFFFF) as failing
expectations.evidence: Correctly identified as failing with 2.32:1 ratio

expectations.text: Correctly identifies disabled button as exempt from contrast requirements
expectations.evidence: Notes inactive UI component exemption for disabled button

expectations.text: Provides specific replacement colour suggestions for failures
expectations.evidence: Suggests #2D7A55 or #1E6B47 for button, #767676 or #595959 for placeholder
Nav links and error messages correctly identified as passing
